title: Op-ed Writing
description: 800 words. One argument. A reason you specifically must write it.
summary_pt_br: Op-ed: um argumento, razão pessoal pra escrever, evidência concreta, implicação pública, 800 palavras.

scenes.subtitle_en: 800 words. One argument. A reason you specifically must write it.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Op-ed: um argumento, razão pessoal pra escrever, evidência concreta, implicação pública, 800 palavras.

scenes.text_en: 800 words. One argument. A reason you specifically must write it. Focus: op-ed writing. An op-ed argues one thing, not three — the pieces that try three fail all three.
scenes.text_pt: Op-ed: um argumento, razão pessoal pra escrever, evidência concreta, implicação pública, 800 palavras. Foco: op-ed writing. Op-ed argumenta um, não três.

scenes.title: Op-ed Writing
scenes.body_en: Op-ed rules: ONE argument (not three). REASON you specifically must write it. CONCRETE evidence, not abstraction. PUBLIC implication. Edit down to 800 words.
scenes.body_pt: Op-ed: um argumento, razão pessoal pra escrever, evidência concreta, implicação pública, 800 palavras.

examples.en: An op-ed argues one thing, not three — the pieces that try three fail all three.
Why you specifically must write this — that's the ethical weight.
examples.pt: Por que você tem que escrever — esse é o peso ético.

examples.en: Edit down to eight hundred words, however tight it hurts.
examples.pt: Corte pra 800 palavras, por mais que doa.

exercise.question: Complete: "An op-ed ___ one thing, not three — the pieces that try three fail all three."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "An op-ed argues one thing, not three — the pieces that try three fail all three."

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
Why you specifically must write this — that's the ethical weight.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Why you specifically must write this — that's the ethical weight."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Por que você tem que escrever — esse é o peso ético.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Op-ed Writing — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre op-ed writing. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Kumar — op-ed draft. What's the argument?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Why must you specifically write it?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I taught in this system for thirty years.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Policy change for the next cohort.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Op-eds that argue one thing land; the rest vanish.

scenes.title: The editorial pitch
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe five op-ed rules. Use at least three op-ed terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: At the newspaper's Thursday op-ed meeting, Kumar submitted a piece he had rewritten for nine straight mornings.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: An op-ed argues one thing, not three — the pieces that try three fail all three, every single time.

paragraphs.speaker: Editor
paragraphs.en: Why you specifically must write this is the ethical weight — thirty years teaching in this exact system gives you standing.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Three concrete cases, no abstractions — the reader trusts specifics far more than they trust any theoretical argument alone.

paragraphs.speaker: Editor
paragraphs.en: Public implication is policy change for the next cohort — and we edit down to eight hundred words, however tight it hurts.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Op-eds that argue one thing land. Op-eds that argue three things vanish — and Kumar taught that lesson every week.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Op-ed Writing". 800 words. One argument. A reason you specifically must write it. An op-ed argues one thing, not three — the pieces that try three fail all three. Why you specifically must write this — that's the ethical weight. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Op-ed Writing". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
